paths.label: Full Course
paths.description: The complete TypeScript & JavaScript curriculum in recommended sequence. Suitable for developers building a solid foundation.

paths.label: Fast Track — TypeScript Focus
paths.description: For developers who already know JavaScript well and want to get productive with TypeScript quickly. Skips JS fundamentals and jumps straight to TypeScript-specific material with just enough JS context.
paths.prerequisites: Comfortable with closures, async/await, array methods, and the event loop

paths.label: Backend Focus — Node.js & TypeScript
paths.description: For developers building APIs and server-side applications with Node.js and TypeScript. Emphasizes async patterns, TypeScript for server code, and Node.js runtime specifics.
paths.notes: M03 and M10 can be read quickly for completeness. M05 (this) is less critical in functional/async Node.js code.

paths.label: Frontend Focus — DOM & React Prep
paths.description: For developers building browser applications. Covers JS fundamentals, async patterns, TypeScript types, and DOM/event APIs needed before learning React or Vue.
paths.notes: M11 (Node.js) can be skipped for pure frontend work but is recommended for full-stack developers.

paths.label: JavaScript Only
paths.description: For developers who need JavaScript proficiency without TypeScript. Covers all JS concepts from variables through Node.js.
paths.notes: M16 (tsconfig) in M07 can be skimmed. TypeScript-specific content in other modules is clearly labeled.
